{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the optimization problem\n\\[\n\\min \\, f(x,y)=(x-1)^2+(y-2)^2 \\qquad \\text{subject to } (x-1)^2=5y,\n\\]\nwhich represents finding the point on the parabola \\(y=\\tfrac{1}{5}(x-1)^2\\) that is closest to \\((1,2)\\) in the Euclidean norm. Among the candidate points, which ones are solutions?"}
